944	/-! ### Entry extraction and the matrix presentation of free-sheaf morphisms
945	
946	(The block below was moved up from the chart-transport section so that the chart-loci
947	covering proof `chartLocus_isOpenCover` — which precedes that section — can use it.) -/
948	
949	/-- The `p`-th **projection of the free sheaf** `O_X^d ⟶ O_X`, through the
950	biproduct/coproduct comparison. Companion of `SheafOfModules.ιFree`; project-local. -/
951	noncomputable def projFree {X : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (p : Fin d) :
952	    SheafOfModules.free (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
953	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv ≫
954	    biproduct.π (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) p
955	
956	/-- The global section of `O_X` carried by an endomorphism of the unit sheaf of modules:
957	the value of the endomorphism at the unit section `1` over `⊤`. This inverts `scalarEnd`
958	(`unitEndSection_scalarEnd`); project-local. -/
959	noncomputable def unitEndSection {X : Scheme.{0}}
960	    (e : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
961	    Γ(X, ⊤) :=
962	  e.val.app (Opposite.op ⊤) (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
963	
964	/-- `unitEndSection` inverts `scalarEnd`: extracting the section of the scalar
965	endomorphism of `a` returns `a`. Project-local. -/
966	lemma unitEndSection_scalarEnd {X : Scheme.{0}} (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
967	    unitEndSection (scalarEnd a) = a := by
968	  rw [unitEndSection, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
969	  -- the restriction along `⊤ ≤ ⊤` is the identity (`homOfLE le_top = 𝟙 ⊤` by proof
970	  -- irrelevance, definitionally)
971	  change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (𝟙 (Opposite.op (⊤ : X.Opens))) a) = a
972	  rw [X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_id]
973	  rfl
974	
975	/-- Every endomorphism of the unit sheaf is the scalar endomorphism of its global
976	section: `scalarEnd (unitEndSection e) = e`. Converse of `unitEndSection_scalarEnd`;
977	together they make `unitEndSection` a bijection. Project-local. -/
978	lemma scalarEnd_unitEndSection {X : Scheme.{0}}
979	    (e : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
980	    scalarEnd (unitEndSection e) = e := by
981	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
982	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
983	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
984	  -- LHS at `Y` is the restriction of `e`'s value at `⊤`; RHS is `e`'s value at `Y`,
985	  -- equal by naturality of `e.val` at `Y ≤ ⊤` (and `1|_Y = 1`).
986	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op
987	      (e.val.app (Opposite.op ⊤) (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)))
988	    = e.val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
989	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply e.val
990	    (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
991	  refine hnat.symm.trans (congrArg (fun z => e.val.app Y z) ?_)
992	  exact PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one X.ringCatSheaf.obj
993	    (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op
994	
995	/-- Composing the `k`-th free injection with the `p`-th free projection is the identity
996	when `k = p` and zero otherwise. Project-local helper for entry extraction. -/
997	lemma ιFree_projFree {X : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (k p : Fin d) :
998	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := X.ringCatSheaf) k ≫ projFree p
999	      = if k = p then 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) else 0 := by
1000	  rw [projFree, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
1001	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
1002	  rw [biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_π]
1003	  by_cases h : k = p
1004	  · subst h; rw [dif_pos rfl, if_pos rfl]; rfl
1005	  · rw [dif_neg h, if_neg h]
1006	
1007	/-- **Entry extraction for `matrixEndRect`**: the `(p, j)` unit-sheaf component of the
1008	rectangular matrix morphism is the scalar endomorphism of the `(p, j)` entry.
1009	Project-local. -/
1010	lemma ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1011	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) (j : Fin r) (p : Fin d) :
1012	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p
1013	      = scalarEnd (M p j) := by
1014	  rw [← Category.assoc, ιFree_matrixEndRect, Preadditive.sum_comp]
1015	  refine (Finset.sum_eq_single p (fun k _ hk => ?_)
1016	    (fun hp => absurd (Finset.mem_univ p) hp)).trans ?_
1017	  · rw [Category.assoc, ιFree_projFree, if_neg hk, Limits.comp_zero]
1018	  · rw [Category.assoc, ιFree_projFree, if_pos rfl, Category.comp_id]
1019	
1020	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1021	/-- **Matrix presentation of a morphism of free sheaves**: any `φ : O_S^r ⟶ O_S^d` is
1022	`matrixEndRect` of its matrix of unit-component sections. Project-local — the
1023	extensionality that lets the pullback-naturality of `matrixEndRect` act on arbitrary
1024	free-sheaf morphisms (such as `chartMatrixHom`). -/
1025	lemma matrixEndRect_unitEndSection {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1026	    (φ : SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
1027	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :
1028	    matrixEndRect (Matrix.of fun p j =>
1029	        unitEndSection (SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ φ ≫ projFree p)) = φ := by
1030	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin r)) _ _ (fun j => ?_)
1031	  simp only [SheafOfModules.freeCofan_inj]
1032	  -- compare the two maps `unit ⟶ free (Fin d)` against the biproduct projections
1033	  refine (cancel_mono (biproduct.isoCoproduct
1034	    (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).inv).mp ?_
1035	  refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
1036	  simp only [Category.assoc]
1037	  -- fold the trailing comparison-projection pair into `projFree p` (definitional)
1038	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect _ ≫ projFree p
1039	    = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ φ ≫ projFree p
1040	  rw [ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree]
1041	  -- reduce the `Matrix.of` entry (definitional) so the extensionality lemma applies
1042	  change scalarEnd (unitEndSection (SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ φ ≫ projFree p))
1043	    = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ φ ≫ projFree p
1044	  exact scalarEnd_unitEndSection _

1132	/-! ### The rectangular matrix calculus: composition, identity, injectivity
1133	
1134	Support for the covering proof `chartLocus_isOpenCover`: the fully rectangular
1135	composition law (generalising `matrixEndRect_comp`), the identity law, injectivity of the
1136	matrix presentation, and the column-restriction law against `freeMap`. -/
1137	
1138	/-- For a square matrix the square and rectangular matrix endomorphisms coincide
1139	(they are definitionally the same biproduct matrix). Project-local bridge. -/
1140	lemma matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
1141	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) : matrixEnd M = matrixEndRect M := rfl
1142	
1143	/-- `matrixEndRect` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
1144	lemma matrixEndRect_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
1145	    matrixEndRect (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ :=
1146	  (matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect _).symm.trans matrixEnd_one

1148	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms, fully rectangular sizes — the
1149	categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEndRect_comp_rect`. -/
1150	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp_rect₂ {S : Scheme.{0}} {n e d : ℕ}
1151	    (mA : Fin n → Fin e →
1152	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf))
1153	    (mB : Fin e → Fin d →
1154	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
1155	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin n => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
1156	        (g := fun _ : Fin e => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mA ≫ biproduct.matrix mB
1157	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mA i p ≫ mB p q) := by
1158	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
1159	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
1160	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
1161	
1162	/-- **Fully rectangular composition law**: `matrixEndRect A ≫ matrixEndRect B =
1163	matrixEndRect (B * A)` for `A : e × n`, `B : d × e` — the matrix product, with the order
1164	reversed by the contravariance of the column/component indexing exactly as in
1165	`matrixEndRect_comp`. Project-local. -/
1166	lemma matrixEndRect_comp_rect {S : Scheme.{0}} {n e d : ℕ}
1167	    (A : Matrix (Fin e) (Fin n) Γ(S, ⊤)) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
1168	    matrixEndRect A ≫ matrixEndRect B = matrixEndRect (B * A) := by
1169	  rw [matrixEndRect, matrixEndRect, matrixEndRect]
1170	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin n) (p : Fin e) => scalarEnd (A p i))
1171	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin e) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (B p i))
1172	      = biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin n) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd ((B * A) p i)) := by
1173	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp_rect₂]
1174	    congr 1
1175	    funext i q
1176	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
1177	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
1178	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
1179	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
1180	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin n) (p : Fin e) => scalarEnd (A p i))),
1181	    hcomp]
1182	
1183	/-- **`matrixEndRect` is injective**: the presenting matrix of a free-sheaf morphism is
1184	unique (entry extraction `ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree` + `unitEndSection_scalarEnd`).
1185	Project-local. -/
1186	lemma matrixEndRect_injective {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1187	    {M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)} (h : matrixEndRect M = matrixEndRect N) :
1188	    M = N := by
1189	  refine Matrix.ext (fun p j => ?_)
1190	  have h1 : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p
1191	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect N ≫ projFree p :=
1192	    congrArg (fun z => SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ z ≫ projFree p) h
1193	  have h2 : scalarEnd (M p j) = scalarEnd (N p j) :=
1194	    (ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree M j p).symm.trans
1195	      (h1.trans (ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree N j p))
1196	  exact (unitEndSection_scalarEnd (M p j)).symm.trans
1197	    ((congrArg unitEndSection h2).trans (unitEndSection_scalarEnd (N p j)))
1198	
1199	/-- **Column restriction against `freeMap`**: precomposing a rectangular matrix morphism
1200	with the index inclusion `freeMap g` is taking the `g`-column submatrix. Project-local. -/
1201	lemma freeMap_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r e : ℕ} (g : Fin e → Fin r)
1202	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
1203	    SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := S.ringCatSheaf) g ≫ matrixEndRect M
1204	      = matrixEndRect (M.submatrix id g) := by
1205	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin e)) _ _ (fun j => ?_)
1206	  simp only [SheafOfModules.freeCofan_inj]
1207	  refine (cancel_mono (biproduct.isoCoproduct
1208	    (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).inv).mp ?_
1209	  refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
1210	  simp only [Category.assoc]
1211	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ (SheafOfModules.freeMap g ≫ matrixEndRect M) ≫ projFree p
1212	    = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect (M.submatrix id g) ≫ projFree p
1213	  calc SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ (SheafOfModules.freeMap g ≫ matrixEndRect M) ≫ projFree p
1214	      = (SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ SheafOfModules.freeMap g) ≫
1215	          matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p := by
1216	        simp only [Category.assoc]
1217	    _ = SheafOfModules.ιFree (g j) ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p :=
1218	        congrArg (· ≫ matrixEndRect M ≫ projFree p)
1219	          (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap (R := S.ringCatSheaf) g j)
1220	    _ = scalarEnd (M p (g j)) := ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree M (g j) p
1221	    _ = SheafOfModules.ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect (M.submatrix id g) ≫ projFree p :=
1222	        (ιFree_matrixEndRect_projFree (M.submatrix id g) j p).symm

1224	/-! ### Epimorphisms between free sheaves split over affines
1225	
1226	The Nakayama/covering step of `chartLocus_isOpenCover` needs pointwise (fibre-level)
1227	surjectivity of an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Over an affine base this needs no
1228	stalk theory at all: the tilde functor `ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules` is fully
1229	faithful and additive, identifies the free sheaf with the tilde of the free module
1230	(`tildeFinsupp`), epimorphisms of modules are surjections, and free modules are
1231	projective — so any epimorphism between free sheaves on `Spec R` is *split*, and its
1232	presenting matrix admits a right inverse over the global sections. -/
1233	
1234	/-- **Epimorphisms between free sheaves of modules on `Spec R` split**: the splitting is
1235	transported through the fully faithful `tilde.functor` from the projectivity of the free
1236	module `Fin d →₀ R`. Project-local. -/
1237	lemma exists_section_of_epi_free_spec {R : CommRingCat.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1238	    (ψ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
1239	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) [Epi ψ] :
1240	    ∃ Φ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
1241	        SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r),
1242	      Φ ≫ ψ = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
1243	  -- the tilde-conjugate of `ψ` and its module-level preimage under full faithfulness
1244	  let ψt : tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R)) ⟶ tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=
1245	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv
1246	  let g : ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R) :=
1247	    tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimage ψt
1248	  have hg : tilde.map g = ψt := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.map_preimage ψt
1249	  haveI hiso_r : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin r)).hom := inferInstance
1250	  haveI hiso_d : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin d)).inv := inferInstance
1251	  haveI hψt : Epi ψt := by
1252	    -- fully explicit: the `(Spec R).Modules` / `(Spec (.of ↥R)).Modules` defeq blocks
1253	    -- automatic instance search across the composite
1254	    haveI h1 : Epi (ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv) :=
1255	      @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ ψ ‹Epi ψ› _ (IsIso.epi_of_iso _)
1256	    exact @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom (IsIso.epi_of_iso _) _ h1
1257	  haveI hge : Epi g := by
1258	    refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
1259	    show Epi (tilde.map g)
1260	    rw [hg]
1261	    exact hψt
1262	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective g.hom := (ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective g).mp hge
1263	  -- a module-level section of `g` by projectivity of the free module
1264	  obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property g.hom LinearMap.id hsurj
1265	  refine ⟨(tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1266	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom, ?_⟩
1267	  have hcomp : ModuleCat.ofHom w ≫ g = 𝟙 (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=
1268	    ModuleCat.hom_ext (by
1269	      rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_id, ModuleCat.hom_ofHom]
1270	      exact hw)
1271	  have hstep : (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ = tilde.map g ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by
1272	    rw [hg]
1273	    simp only [ψt, Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
1274	  calc ((tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1275	        (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom) ≫ ψ
1276	      = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1277	        ((tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ) := by simp only [Category.assoc]
1278	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w) ≫
1279	        tilde.map g ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by rw [hstep]
1280	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom w ≫ g) ≫
1281	        (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by rw [← tilde.map_comp_assoc]
1282	    _ = (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv ≫ 𝟙 _ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).hom := by
1283	        rw [hcomp, tilde.map_id]
1284	    _ = 𝟙 _ := by rw [Category.id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id]
1285	
1286	/-- **Presenting matrices of epimorphisms admit right inverses over `Spec R`**: combine
1287	the splitting `exists_section_of_epi_free_spec` with the matrix presentation of the
1288	section and the rectangular composition law. Project-local. -/
1289	lemma exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec {R : CommRingCat.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1290	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(Spec R, ⊤)) (h : Epi (matrixEndRect M)) :
1291	    ∃ G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) Γ(Spec R, ⊤), M * G = 1 := by
1292	  haveI := h
1293	  obtain ⟨Φ, hΦ⟩ := exists_section_of_epi_free_spec (matrixEndRect M)
1294	  refine ⟨Matrix.of (fun q p => unitEndSection
1295	    (SheafOfModules.ιFree p ≫ Φ ≫ projFree q)), ?_⟩
1296	  have hpres : matrixEndRect (Matrix.of (fun q p => unitEndSection
1297	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree p ≫ Φ ≫ projFree q))) = Φ := matrixEndRect_unitEndSection Φ
1298	  apply matrixEndRect_injective
1299	  rw [← matrixEndRect_comp_rect, hpres, hΦ, matrixEndRect_one]
1300	
1301	/-- **Presenting matrices of epimorphisms admit right inverses over any affine scheme** —
1302	the `Spec` case transported along `S.isoSpec` through the free-pullback comparisons
1303	(`pullback_conj_matrixEndRect`). Project-local; the algebraic heart of the Nakayama
1304	covering step. -/
1305	lemma exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} [IsAffine S] {d r : ℕ}
1306	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) (h : Epi (matrixEndRect M)) :
1307	    ∃ G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤), M * G = 1 := by
1308	  -- transport to `Spec Γ(S, ⊤)` along the inverse of `isoSpec`
1309	  haveI h1 : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M)) :=
1310	    @CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv)
1311	      inferInstance _ _ _ h
1312	  haveI h2 : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin r)).inv ≫
1313	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M) ≫
1314	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin d)).hom) := by
1315	    haveI : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullback S.isoSpec.inv).map (matrixEndRect M) ≫
1316	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso S.isoSpec.inv (Fin d)).hom) := epi_comp _ _
1317	    exact epi_comp _ _
1318	  have h3 : Epi (matrixEndRect (M.map
1319	      ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv)))) := by
1320	    rw [← pullback_conj_matrixEndRect S.isoSpec.inv M]
1321	    exact h2
1322	  obtain ⟨G', hG'⟩ := exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec
1323	    (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))) h3
1324	  -- pull the right inverse back along the (iso) global-sections comorphism
1325	  have hcancel : Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom
1326	      = 𝟙 (Γ(S, ⊤)) := by
1327	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, S.isoSpec.hom_inv_id]
1328	    simp
1329	  refine ⟨G'.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)), ?_⟩
1330	  have hM : (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))).map
1331	      ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) = M := by
1332	    rw [Matrix.map_map]
1333	    refine Matrix.ext (fun p j => ?_)
1334	    have hpt := congrArg (fun (z : Γ(S, ⊤) ⟶ Γ(S, ⊤)) =>
1335	      (CommRingCat.Hom.hom z) (M p j)) hcancel
1336	    simpa using hpt
1337	  calc M * G'.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom))
1338	      = (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))).map
1339	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) *
1340	        G'.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) := by rw [hM]
1341	    _ = ((M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.inv))) * G').map
1342	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) :=
1343	        Matrix.map_mul.symm
1344	    _ = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _).map
1345	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop S.isoSpec.hom)) := by rw [hG']
1346	    _ = 1 := Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)

1352	/-- **Transport of a matrix automorphism through `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`** — the
1353	reusable (a)→(c) bridge for the bundle cocycle (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_transport`). A
1354	transition isomorphism of the bundle-transition shape
1355	`pullbackFreeIso a ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso b).symm` (a `GL_d` matrix
1356	automorphism conjugated to the overlap pullbacks) transports along `p : W ⟶ V` to the same
1357	shape over `p ≫ a` / `p ≫ b`, with the matrix base-changed entrywise by the comorphism
1358	`p.appTop`. Combines the matrix-naturality atom `matrixEnd_pullback` with the free-pullback
1359	pseudofunctor coherence `Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp`. Project-local — this is the
1360	abstract core of the bundle cocycle transport, independent of the Grassmannian charts. -/
1361	lemma pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V)
1362	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{0}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj) {d : ℕ}
1363	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(V, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
1364	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b
1365	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d) ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM ≪≫
1366	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).symm)).hom
1367	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom ≫
1368	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
1369	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
1370	  simp only [Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom,
1371	    Iso.symm_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom]
1372	  -- Front coherence: the `pullbackComp` cast + the `a`-leg comparison assemble into the
1373	  -- composite free-pullback comparison `Q_{p≫a}` (pseudofunctoriality, `pullbackFreeIso_comp`).
1374	  have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
1375	          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom ≫
1376	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d)).hom ≫
1377	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
1378	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom := by
1379	    erw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp a p (Fin d)]
1380	    simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
1381	    rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc]
1382	  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into the
1383	  -- inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`. Derived by inverting the `b`-leg coherence iso.
1384	  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv ≫
1385	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).inv ≫
1386	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom
1387	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
1388	    have hiso : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d)) ≪≫
1389	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)
1390	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)) ≪≫
1391	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d) := by
1392	      apply Iso.ext
1393	      simpa using Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp b p (Fin d)
1394	    have hinv := congrArg Iso.inv hiso
1395	    simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv] at hinv
1396	    -- hinv : Q_{p≫b}.inv ≫ Cpb.inv.app free = Q_p.inv ≫ (pullback p).map Q_b.inv
1397	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Iso.app_hom]
1398	    erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
1399	    rw [Category.comp_id]
1400	  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated matrix automorphism and apply the matrix atom.
1401	  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, matrixEnd_pullback]
1402	  -- Expand both comparison legs of the target via the coherences `hfront`/`hback`; the two sides
1403	  -- then coincide up to the (here definitional) associativity of the composite.
1404	  rw [← hfront, ← hback]
1405	  rfl

1407	/-! ### The base-change bridge (b): geometric comorphisms = localised cocycle ring homs
1408	
1409	The three scheme-pullback base-change maps `Γ(U^I_J,⊤) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK,⊤)` — induced by the two
1410	pullback projections and the triple transition `t'` — are identified, through the affine
1411	global-sections isomorphism `ΓSpecIso` and the away-pullback identification
1412	`V_IJK ≅ Spec S_I` (`awayPullbackIso`), with the ring homomorphisms `awayInclLeft`,
1413	`awayInclRight` and `cocycleΘIJ ∘ awayInclRight` over which the matrix cocycle L1
1414	(`bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix`) is stated. -/
1415	
1416	/-- **Affine global-sections comorphism is the inducing ring homomorphism**
1417	(`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec`): for a ring homomorphism `φ : A ⟶ B`, the
1418	global-sections comorphism of `Spec.map φ`, conjugated through the counit isomorphisms
1419	`ΓSpecIso`, is `φ` itself. Pure `Γ ⊣ Spec` naturality; project-local packaging. -/
1420	lemma baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec {A B : CommRingCat.{0}} (φ : A ⟶ B) :
1421	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop (Spec.map φ)
1422	      = φ ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso B).inv := by
1423	  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, Category.assoc,
1424	    Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
1425	
1426	/-- The global-sections identification of the triple overlap: the ring map from the
1427	triple-overlap coordinate ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]` to the global sections of the
1428	scheme-level triple overlap `V_IJK = U^I_J ×_{U^I} U^I_K`, namely the affine identification
1429	`ΓSpecIso` transported through the away-pullback identification `awayPullbackIso`. It is the
1430	common codomain conjugation of the three base-change bridges below. Project-local. -/
1431	noncomputable def tripleOverlapSections (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1432	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :

1745	  -- transpose of the inner chain along `τ ≫ (f_pq ≫ ι_p)`: fold the unit pair

1760	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i))

1765	      (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)) (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p)

1772	    (W := W)

1775	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i))).map

1781	      ((pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) ≫ D.ι i))

1800	          (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p)).inv.app W ≫

1821	/-- **Transpose of the first descent-leg factor** along the composite overlap
1822	immersion `f_pq ≫ ι_p`: the `aComp`-factor of `glueLegA` (unit along `f_pq` +

1824	the geometric counit. Instance of `homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp` at the counit
1825	(whose transpose is the identity, by the right triangle). Project-local. -/
1826	lemma glueLegA_component_transpose (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (p q : D.J)
1827	    (Mp : (D.U p).Modules) :
1828	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p)).homEquiv
1829	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p)).obj Mp)
1830	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p q)).obj Mp)).symm
1831	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p)).map
1832	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p q)).unit.app Mp) ≫
1833	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p q) (D.ι p)).hom.app
1834	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p q)).obj Mp))
1835	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.f p q) (D.ι p)).inv.app
1836	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p)).obj Mp) ≫
1837	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p q)).map
1838	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι p)).counit.app Mp) := by
1839	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
1840	  have hε : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι p)).homEquiv
1841	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p)).obj Mp) Mp
1842	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι p)).counit.app Mp)
1843	      = 𝟙 ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p)).obj Mp) :=
1844	    (Adjunction.homEquiv_unit _ _ _ _).trans
1845	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι p)).right_triangle_components
1846	        Mp)
1847	  have h := homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp (D.f p q) (D.ι p)
1848	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι p)).counit.app Mp)
1849	  -- `rw [hε] at h` cannot match (the counit codomain carries a `𝟭`-wrapper); term-mode
1850	  exact (Category.id_comp _).symm.trans ((eq_whisker hε _).symm.trans h)

1852	/-! ### Pseudofunctor cast coherence for the conjugated cocycle
1853	
1854	The remaining content of the descent obligation (`glueChartComponent_leg_compat`) is a
1855	comparison of two regroupings of iterated pullbacks: the route through the glued scheme
1856	(the triple-square casts) against the route through the chart overlaps (the pair-square
1857	casts whiskered by the triple-overlap projections). This comparison is *pure cast
1858	coherence*: it involves only the pseudofunctor comparisons `pullbackComp` and the
1859	congruence casts `pullbackCongr` — no units, counits, or transition isomorphisms. The
1860	block below derives it (`pullback_cast_compat`) from the Mathlib associativity
1861	2-cocycle `Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity` specialised to objects, plus a
1862	handful of generic `subst`-style `pullbackComp`/`pullbackCongr` compatibilities. -/
1863	
1864	section CastCoherence
1865	
1866	/-- Generic 4-factor rearrangement: solve an iso-chain equation for its last factor.
1867	Project-local helper for the solved forms of the associativity cocycle. -/
1868	private lemma comp4_solve_last {𝒞 : Type*} [Category 𝒞] {a b c d e : 𝒞}
1869	    (A : a ≅ b) (B : b ≅ c) (Cc : c ≅ d) {D : d ⟶ e} {E : a ⟶ e}
1870	    (hE : A.hom ≫ B.hom ≫ Cc.hom ≫ D = E) :
1871	    D = Cc.inv ≫ B.inv ≫ A.inv ≫ E := by
1872	  rw [← hE]; simp
1873	
1874	/-- Generic 4-factor rearrangement: solve an iso-chain equation for its middle pair.
1875	Project-local. -/
1876	private lemma comp4_solve_mid {𝒞 : Type*} [Category 𝒞] {a b c d e : 𝒞}
1877	    (A : a ≅ b) {B : b ⟶ c} {Cc : c ⟶ d} (D : d ≅ e) {E : a ⟶ e}
1878	    (hE : A.hom ≫ B ≫ Cc ≫ D.hom = E) :
1879	    B ≫ Cc = A.inv ≫ E ≫ D.inv := by
1880	  rw [← hE]; simp
1881	
1882	/-- Generic 4-factor rearrangement: solve an iso-chain equation for its front pair.
1883	Project-local. -/
1884	private lemma comp4_solve_front {𝒞 : Type*} [Category 𝒞] {a b c d e : 𝒞}
1885	    {A : a ⟶ b} {B : b ⟶ c} (Cc : c ≅ d) (D : d ≅ e) {E : a ⟶ e}
1886	    (hE : A ≫ B ≫ Cc.hom ≫ D.hom = E) :
1887	    A ≫ B = E ≫ D.inv ≫ Cc.inv := by
1888	  rw [← hE]; simp
1889	
1890	/-- `pullbackCongr` evaluated at an object, inverse side: the `eqToHom` of the
1891	object-level equality. Generic `subst` companion of `pullbackCongr_hom_app_eqToHom`.
1892	Project-local. -/
1893	lemma pullbackCongr_inv_app_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
1894	    (N : T.Modules) :
1895	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).inv.app N
1896	      = eqToHom (congrArg (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj N) h.symm) := by
1897	  subst h
1898	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
1899	
1900	/-- The pseudofunctor associativity 2-cocycle of module pullback, at an object: the
1901	two regroupings of a triple composite of pullbacks differ by the congruence cast along
1902	`Category.assoc`. Object-level form of `Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity`.
1903	Project-local. -/
1904	lemma pullbackComp_assoc_app {X' Y' Z' T' : Scheme.{u}} (f : X' ⟶ Y') (g : Y' ⟶ Z')
1905	    (h : Z' ⟶ T') (M : T'.Modules) :
1906	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv.app M ≫
1907	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g h).inv.app M) ≫
1908	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj M) ≫
1909	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom.app M
1910	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc f g h).symm).hom.app M := by
1911	  have e := congrArg (fun α => α.app M)
1912	    (Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := f) (g := g) (h := h))
1913	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
1914	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app] at e
1915	  rw [pullbackCongr_hom_app_eqToHom]
1916	  exact e
1917	
1918	/-- Solved form of the associativity cocycle at a composite first leg: the regrouping
1919	comparison `((f ≫ g) ≫ h)^*` factors through the two inner comparisons and the
1920	associativity cast. Project-local. -/
1921	@[reassoc]
1922	lemma pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app {X' Y' Z' T' : Scheme.{u}} (f : X' ⟶ Y')
1923	    (g : Y' ⟶ Z') (h : Z' ⟶ T') (M : T'.Modules) :
1924	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom.app M
1925	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj M) ≫
1926	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g h).hom.app M) ≫
1927	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).hom.app M ≫
1928	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc f g h).symm).hom.app M :=
1929	  comp4_solve_last ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).app M).symm
1930	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g h).app M)).symm
1931	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj M))
1932	    (pullbackComp_assoc_app f g h M)
1933	
1934	/-- Solved form of the associativity cocycle for the middle pair: pulling back the
1935	inner regrouping inverse and regrouping the outer pair equals the comparison at the
1936	composite second leg, the associativity cast, and the ungrouping at the composite
1937	first leg. Project-local. -/
1938	@[reassoc]
1939	lemma pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app {X' Y' Z' T' : Scheme.{u}} (f : X' ⟶ Y')
1940	    (g : Y' ⟶ Z') (h : Z' ⟶ T') (M : T'.Modules) :
1941	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g h).inv.app M) ≫
1942	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj M)
1943	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).hom.app M ≫
1944	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc f g h).symm).hom.app M ≫
1945	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).inv.app M :=
1946	  ((comp4_solve_mid ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).app M).symm
1947	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).app M)
1948	    (pullbackComp_assoc_app f g h M)).trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm).trans
1949	    (Category.assoc _ _ _)
1950	
1951	/-- Solved form of the associativity cocycle for the front pair: ungrouping at the
1952	composite second leg followed by the pulled-back inner ungrouping equals the
1953	associativity cast, the ungrouping at the composite first leg, and the outer
1954	ungrouping. Project-local. -/
1955	lemma pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app {X' Y' Z' T' : Scheme.{u}} (f : X' ⟶ Y')
1956	    (g : Y' ⟶ Z') (h : Z' ⟶ T') (M : T'.Modules) :
1957	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv.app M ≫
1958	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g h).inv.app M)
1959	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc f g h).symm).hom.app M ≫
1960	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).inv.app M ≫
1961	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj M) :=
1962	  comp4_solve_front
1963	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f g).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj M))
1964	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).app M)
1965	    (pullbackComp_assoc_app f g h M)

1969	@[reassoc]
1970	lemma pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app {X' Y' Z' : Scheme.{u}} (f : X' ⟶ Y')
1971	    {x y : Y' ⟶ Z'} (h : x = y) (W : Z'.Modules) :

1974	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f y).hom.app W ≫
1975	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
1976	          (show f ≫ x = f ≫ y from by rw [h])).inv.app W := by
1977	  subst h
1978	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]

2018	/-- Generic 5-factor pipeline rearrangement: substitute a middle-pair regrouping, a
2019	front-pair exchange and a back-pair exchange in one move. Stated in an abstract
2020	category (where `rw` matching is reliable) and applied to the concrete pullback chain
2021	by unification — the lever for the `X.Modules` instance diamond. Project-local. -/
2022	private lemma comp5_rearrange {𝒞 : Type*} [Category 𝒞]
2023	    {x₀ x₁ x₂ x₃ x₄ x₅ y₁ y₂ z₁ w : 𝒞}
2024	    {d₃ : x₀ ⟶ x₁} {d₄ : x₁ ⟶ x₂} {d₅ : x₂ ⟶ x₃} {d₆ : x₃ ⟶ x₄} {d₇ : x₄ ⟶ x₅}
2025	    {B : x₁ ⟶ y₁} {cC : y₁ ⟶ y₂} {e : y₂ ⟶ x₃} {A : x₀ ⟶ z₁} {cD : z₁ ⟶ y₁}

2029	  rw [reassoc_of% h45, reassoc_of% h6, ← Category.assoc d₃ B, h3, Category.assoc]

2031	/-- **Cast compatibility of the triple-overlap and pair-overlap regroupings** (the
2032	coherence core of the conjugated cocycle): given a pair square `t ≫ κ = a ≫ ι`, a
2033	middle bridge `u ≫ t = τ ≫ b` and the induced triple square
2034	`τ ≫ (b ≫ κ) = (u ≫ a) ≫ ι`, the cast chain through the triple square equals the
2035	pair-square cast chain pulled back along `u`, re-bracketed through the pseudofunctor
2036	associativity. Pure cast coherence — no units, counits, or transition isomorphisms.
2037	Project-local. -/
2038	lemma pullback_cast_compat {P A' B' C' E' X' : Scheme.{u}}
2039	    (u : P ⟶ A') (a : A' ⟶ C') (ι : C' ⟶ X') (t : A' ⟶ E') (κ : E' ⟶ X')
2040	    (τ : P ⟶ B') (b : B' ⟶ E')
2041	    (hpair : t ≫ κ = a ≫ ι) (hmid : u ≫ t = τ ≫ b)
2042	    (htriple : τ ≫ (b ≫ κ) = (u ≫ a) ≫ ι) (W : X'.Modules) :
2043	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (u ≫ a) ι).hom.app W ≫
2044	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr htriple).inv.app W ≫
2045	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp τ (b ≫ κ)).inv.app W ≫
2046	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback τ).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b κ).inv.app W)
2047	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp u a).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ι).obj W) ≫
2048	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback u).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a ι).hom.app W) ≫
2049	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback u).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hpair).inv.app W) ≫
2050	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback u).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp t κ).inv.app W) ≫
2051	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp u t).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback κ).obj W) ≫
2052	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hmid).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback κ).obj W) ≫
2053	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp τ b).inv.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback κ).obj W) := by
2054	  -- regroup the composite first leg on the left-hand side (`erw`: plain `rw` cannot
2055	  -- match the comp-node pattern under the `X.Modules` instance diamond)
2056	  erw [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app_assoc u a ι W]
2057	  -- regroup the τ-side trailing pair on the left-hand side
2058	  rw [pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app τ b κ W]
2059	  -- strip the common two-factor prefix
2060	  refine whisker_eq _ (whisker_eq _ ?_)
2061	  -- the residual three-cast stacks agree under any continuation (all `pullbackCongr`
2062	  -- casts are `eqToHom`s; the fresh `have` is elaborated uniformly, so `simp` fires)
2063	  have hcast : ∀ {Z : P.Modules}
2064	      (zc : (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((τ ≫ b) ≫ κ)).obj W ⟶ Z),
2065	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc u a ι).symm).hom.app W ≫
2066	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr htriple).inv.app W ≫
2067	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc τ b κ).symm).hom.app W ≫ zc
2068	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
2069	              (show u ≫ t ≫ κ = u ≫ a ≫ ι by rw [hpair])).inv.app W ≫
2070	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Category.assoc u t κ).symm).hom.app W ≫
2071	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
2072	              (show (u ≫ t) ≫ κ = (τ ≫ b) ≫ κ by rw [hmid])).hom.app W ≫ zc := by
2073	    intro Z zc
2074	    simp only [pullbackCongr_hom_app_eqToHom, pullbackCongr_inv_app_eqToHom,
2075	      eqToHom_trans_assoc]
2076	  -- right-hand side: regroup the middle pair (t, κ), commute the pair congruence past
2077	  -- the regrouping (second argument) and the middle bridge congruence past the
2078	  -- regrouping (first argument) — assembled by the abstract-category rearrangement
2079	  -- (the corresponding `rw`s cannot match these comp nodes under the diamond)
2080	  exact (whisker_eq _ (hcast _)).trans
2081	    (comp5_rearrange (pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app u t κ W)
2082	      (pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app u hpair W)
2083	      (pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app hmid κ W)).symm
2084

2086	in abstract categories and applied by unification (the `X.Modules` diamond lever).
2087	Project-local. -/
2088	private lemma map_fold₅ {𝒞 𝒟 : Type*} [Category 𝒞] [Category 𝒟] (F : 𝒞 ⥤ 𝒟)
2089	    {x₀ x₁ x₂ x₃ x₄ x₅ : 𝒞} {a : x₀ ⟶ x₁} {k₁ : x₁ ⟶ x₂} {k₂ : x₂ ⟶ x₃} {k₃ : x₃ ⟶ x₄}
2090	    {k₄ : x₄ ⟶ x₅} {z : x₀ ⟶ x₅} (h : a ≫ k₁ ≫ k₂ ≫ k₃ ≫ k₄ = z) :
2091	    F.map a ≫ F.map k₁ ≫ F.map k₂ ≫ F.map k₃ ≫ F.map k₄ = F.map z := by
2092	  rw [← F.map_comp, ← F.map_comp, ← F.map_comp, ← F.map_comp, h]

2095	coherence (`hcast`), migrate the counit decoration `Y` through the three trailing
2096	comparisons by the naturality squares `nY1`–`nY3`, peel the chart component off the
2097	front by `nX`, and fold the chart-level collapse `hfold`. Pure rebracketing in an

2100	private lemma side_collapse_left {𝒞 : Type*} [Category 𝒞]
2101	    {x₀ x₁ y₁ y₂ y₃ y₄ y₅ a₁ a₂ a₃ a₄ a₅ a₆ b₁ m₁ m₂ m₃ : 𝒞}
2102	    {X : x₀ ⟶ x₁} {c₁ : x₁ ⟶ y₁} {c₂ : y₁ ⟶ y₂} {c₃ : y₂ ⟶ y₃} {w : y₃ ⟶ y₅}
2103	    {c₄ : y₃ ⟶ y₄} {Y : y₄ ⟶ y₅}
2104	    {F1 : x₁ ⟶ a₁} {F2 : a₁ ⟶ a₂} {F3 : a₂ ⟶ a₃} {F4 : a₃ ⟶ a₄} {F5 : a₄ ⟶ a₅}
2105	    {F6 : a₅ ⟶ a₆} {F7 : a₆ ⟶ y₄}
2106	    {F1' : x₀ ⟶ b₁} {X' : b₁ ⟶ a₁}
2107	    {Y₁ : a₆ ⟶ m₁} {F7' : m₁ ⟶ y₅} {Y₂ : a₅ ⟶ m₂} {F6' : m₂ ⟶ m₁}
2108	    {Y₃ : a₄ ⟶ m₃} {F5' : m₃ ⟶ m₂} {Z : b₁ ⟶ m₃}
2109	    (hw : w = c₄ ≫ Y)
2110	    (hcast : c₁ ≫ c₂ ≫ c₃ ≫ c₄ = F1 ≫ F2 ≫ F3 ≫ F4 ≫ F5 ≫ F6 ≫ F7)
2111	    (nX : X ≫ F1 = F1' ≫ X')
2112	    (nY1 : F7 ≫ Y = Y₁ ≫ F7')
2113	    (nY2 : F6 ≫ Y₁ = Y₂ ≫ F6')
2114	    (nY3 : F5 ≫ Y₂ = Y₃ ≫ F5')
2115	    (hfold : X' ≫ F2 ≫ F3 ≫ F4 ≫ Y₃ = Z) :

2118	  calc X ≫ c₁ ≫ c₂ ≫ c₃ ≫ c₄ ≫ Y

2120	    _ = X ≫ (F1 ≫ F2 ≫ F3 ≫ F4 ≫ F5 ≫ F6 ≫ F7) ≫ Y := by rw [hcast]
2121	    _ = X ≫ F1 ≫ F2 ≫ F3 ≫ F4 ≫ F5 ≫ F6 ≫ F7 ≫ Y := by simp only [Category.assoc]
2122	    _ = F1' ≫ X' ≫ F2 ≫ F3 ≫ F4 ≫ F5 ≫ F6 ≫ Y₁ ≫ F7' := by

2136	    {x₀ x₁ s₁ s₂ y₁ y₂ y₃ z₁ z₂ z₃ y₆ e₁ a₁ a₂ a₃ a₄ a₅ a₆ b₀ b₁ m₁ m₂ m₃ : 𝒞}
2137	    {X : x₀ ⟶ x₁} {c₁ : x₁ ⟶ y₁} {c₂ : y₁ ⟶ y₂} {c₃ : y₂ ⟶ y₃} {w : y₃ ⟶ y₆}
2138	    {d₁ : y₃ ⟶ z₁} {d₂ : z₁ ⟶ z₂} {d₃ : z₂ ⟶ z₃} {d₄ : z₃ ⟶ y₆}

2140	    {ctq : s₂ ⟶ e₁}
2141	    {G1 : s₁ ⟶ a₁} {G2 : a₁ ⟶ a₂} {G3 : a₂ ⟶ a₃} {G4 : a₃ ⟶ a₄} {G5 : a₄ ⟶ a₅}
2142	    {G6 : a₅ ⟶ a₆} {G7 : a₆ ⟶ z₂}
2143	    {Cs' : x₀ ⟶ b₀} {X₁ : b₀ ⟶ s₁} {G1' : b₀ ⟶ b₁} {X₂ : b₁ ⟶ a₁}

2146	    (hw : w = d₁ ≫ d₂ ≫ d₃ ≫ d₄)
2147	    (hc₁ : c₁ = Cs ≫ c₁' ≫ ce₁)
2148	    (hex : c₃ ≫ d₁ = ce₂ ≫ c₃')

2185	    {x₀ l₁ l₂ l₃ l₄ l₅ e₁ e₂ e₃ r₁ r₂ r₃ : 𝒞}

2197	/-! ### The candidate inverse of the chart restriction morphism
2198	
2199	The inverse `σ_i : M i ⟶ ι_i^* (glue …)` is assembled as an equalizer lift: the chart
2200	pullback preserves the descent equalizer (`glueRestrictEqualizerIso`) and the
2201	pushforward product (`glueRestrictProdIso`), so a map into `ι_i^* (glue …)` is a
2202	family of maps into the factors `ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)` equalizing the restricted legs.
2203	The `j`-th component (`glueChartComponent`) transports a section of `M i` to the
2204	overlap: the unit along `f_ij`, the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, then the inverse of
2205	the overlap base change `β_ij` (`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`, in pullback form
2206	`glueOverlapFactorIso`). The three named obligations consuming the cocycle hypotheses
2207	are `glueChartFamily_equalizes` (C2 transported), `glueChartComponent_self_counit`
2208	(C1 + the counit triangle), and `glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent` (the
2209	pair-`(i,j)` equalizer condition transposed). -/

2211	section GlueRestrictionInverse
2212	
2213	variable (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
2214	
2215	/-- **Object-level overlap base change in pullback form**: the `β_ij` of
2216	`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`, evaluated at `M j` and conjugated through
2217	`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` on both sides, identifying
2218	`ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j) ≅ (f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` with the geometric pullback
2219	functors. Project-local. -/
2220	noncomputable def glueOverlapFactorIso (i j : D.J) :
2221	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j))
2222	      ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f i j)).obj
2223	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) :=
2224	  (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)).symm.app
2225	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j)) ≪≫
2226	    (glueOverlapBaseChangeIso D i j).app (M j) ≪≫
2227	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f i j)).mapIso
2228	      ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).app (M j))
2229	
2230	variable (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
2231	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
2232	
2233	/-- **The `j`-th component of the candidate inverse** `σ_i`: transport a section of
2234	`M i` to the overlap `V_ij` (the unit of the pullback–pushforward adjunction along
2235	`f_ij`), across the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, and back up through the inverse of
2236	the overlap base change `β_ij`. Project-local. -/
2237	noncomputable def glueChartComponent (i j : D.J) :
2238	    M i ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj
2239	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j)) :=
2240	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f i j)).unit.app (M i) ≫
2241	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f i j)).map (g i j).hom ≫
2242	    (glueOverlapFactorIso D M i j).inv

2245	`glueChartComponent`s assembled through the product-preservation comparison
2246	`glueRestrictProdIso`. Project-local. -/
2247	noncomputable def glueChartFamily (i : D.J) :
2248	    M i ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glueProd D M) :=
2249	  Pi.lift (glueChartComponent D M g i) ≫ (glueRestrictProdIso D M i).inv
2250	
2251	/-- **Transpose of the second descent-leg factor** along the composite overlap
2252	immersion `f_pq ≫ ι_p`: the `bComp`-factor of `glueLegB` (unit along `t_pq ≫ f_qp`,

2257	lemma glueLegB_component_transpose (p q : D.J) :
2258	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p)).homEquiv
2259	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι q)).obj (M q))
2260	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p q)).obj (M p))).symm
2261	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι q)).map
2262	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
2263	              (D.t p q ≫ D.f q p)).unit.app (M q)) ≫
2264	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p q ≫ D.f q p) (D.ι q)).hom.app
2265	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p q ≫ D.f q p)).obj (M q)) ≫
2266	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
2267	            ((D.t p q ≫ D.f q p) ≫ D.ι q)).map (g p q).inv ≫

2223	/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
2224	surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
2225	the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`. Since `universalQuotient` is the descent
2226	equalizer of pushforwards, the morphism is `glueLift` of the per-chart adjoint
2227	transposes (`tautologicalQuotientComponent`); the equalizing condition — the chart
2228	compatibility `g_{IJ} ∘ f_{IJ}^* u^I = (t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI})^* u^J`, whose matrix content is
2229	`X^J = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` / `imageMatrix`) — is
2230	`tautologicalQuotient_overlap`, transposed through the adjunction by
2231	`tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose`. -/
2232	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
2233	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
2234	  Scheme.Modules.glueLift (theGlueData d r)
2235	    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
2236	    (bundleTransitionData d r)
2237	    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
2238	    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K)
2239	    (fun I => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
2240	    (fun p => (tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose d r p.1 p.2).mpr
2241	      (tautologicalQuotient_overlap d r p.1 p.2))

2243	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
2244	
2245	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
2246	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
2247	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
2248	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
2249	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
2250	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
2251	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
2252	
2253	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
2254	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
2255	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
2256	unchanged. -/
2257	
2258	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
2259	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
2260	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
2261	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
2262	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
2263	  F : T.Modules
2264	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
2265	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
2266	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
2267	  epi : Epi q
2268	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
2269	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
2270	
2271	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
2272	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
2273	coincide). -/

2338	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
2339	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
2340	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
2341	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
2342	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
2343	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
2344	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
2345	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
2346	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
2347	  map_id X := by
2348	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
2349	    ext z
2350	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
2351	    | _ x =>
2352	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
2353	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
2354	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
2355	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
2356	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
2357	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
2358	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
2359	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
2360	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
2361	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫

2364	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]

2368	    ext z
2369	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
2370	    | _ x =>

2372	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))

2481	/-! ### The Nitsure §1 inverse construction: chart loci, chart matrices, chart morphisms
2482	
2483	For a rank-`d` quotient `x = ⟨F, q⟩` on `T` and a size-`d` subset `I ⊆ Fin r`, the

2857	PROOF (the blueprint's stalk-wise route, `lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map`): being an
2858	isomorphism of sheaves of modules is stalk-local. Each point of the iso-locus has an
2859	open neighbourhood `U` on which `φ` pulls back to an isomorphism; restriction along the
2860	open immersion `U.ι` preserves stalks (`Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso`), so the

2862	same stalk comparison for the locus inclusion shows the restriction of `φ` to the locus

2864	(`TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`); `toPresheaf` reflects it back to
2865	`X.Modules`, and `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` transports it to the pullback functor. -/
2866	theorem isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N) :
2867	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (isoLocus φ).ι).map φ) := by
2868	  -- Step 1: the stalk of `φ` (as a map of abelian presheaves) is invertible at every
2869	  -- point of the iso-locus.

2871	      IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab t).map
2872	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
2873	    intro t ht

3878	/-! ### Overlap compatibility of the chart morphisms (Nitsure §1 gluing step)
3879	
3880	On the intersection `P = T_I ×_T T_J` of two chart loci both chart composites pull back
3881	to isomorphisms, so the pulled-back quotient is presented by both matrices `M^I_P` and
3882	`M^J_P`. The change-of-basis identity `M^I_P = (M^I_P)_J · M^J_P`
3883	(`presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis`) identifies the `J`-classifying ring map with the
3884	localization-transport `lift(aeval M^I_P) ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` of the `I`-classifying map, so the
3885	two chart morphisms agree into the glued scheme by the glue condition (`chart_point_eq`,
3886	the CommRing form of `existence_chart_kpoint_eq`). The block below supplies the
3887	transports: invertibility along a composite (`isIso_pullback_map_comp`), the presented
3888	matrix along a composite (`presentedMatrix_comp`) and along an equality of base
3889	morphisms (`presentedMatrix_congr`), its own minor (`presentedMatrix_submatrix_self`),
3890	the realisation `aeval(M^I)(X^I) = M^I` (`universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix`), the
3891	image-matrix transport (`imageMatrix_map_ringHom`), and chart-morphism naturality
3892	(`comp_chartMorphism`). -/

3899	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).map c) := by
3900	  haveI h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).map c)) :=
3901	    Functor.map_isIso _ _
3902	  haveI h2 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map c) := h1
3903	  exact (NatIso.isIso_map_iff (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a) c).mp h2

4626	lemma presentedMatrix_rqPullback {T' T W : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) {r d : ℕ}

4650	      = e.hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r)) ≫

4926	/-! ### The two inverse laws of the universal property
4927	
4928	Uniqueness (`left_inv`): over the preimage of each chart-immersion range the glued
4929	morphism and `ψ` are both classified by the `ψ`-image of the universal matrix
4930	(`presentedMatrix_rqPullback` + `presentedMatrix_tautological`), so they agree on an

4932	tautological quotient and `x.q` are presented by the same matrix
4933	(`presentedMatrix_rqPullback_grPoint`), so each kernel annihilates the opposite
4934	quotient (`pullback_map_cover_faithful`); the two `Abelian.epiDesc` descents are then
4935	mutually inverse by epi-cancellation, witnessing the equivalence `Rel`. -/

5459	/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
5460	the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
5461	quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.
5462	
5463	The forward map sends `ψ : T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` to the pullback `ψ^*(U, u)` of the tautological
5464	pair (`tautologicalRankQuotient`); naturality (`homEquiv_comp`) is the already-proven
5465	pseudofunctoriality `(functor d r).map_comp` evaluated at the tautological point. The
5466	inverse is the chart-by-chart construction `grPointOfRankQuotient`; the two inverse laws
5467	are the remaining content (they consume the chart restriction isomorphisms
5468	`universalQuotient_restrictionIso` and the glued-scheme universal property). -/
5469	noncomputable def represents (d r : ℕ) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
5470	    (functor d r).RepresentableBy (scheme d r) where
5471	  homEquiv {T} :=
5472	    { toFun := fun ψ => Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback ψ (tautologicalRankQuotient d r))
5473	      invFun := Quotient.lift (grPointOfRankQuotient d r)
5474	        (fun _ _ h => grPointOfRankQuotient_rel d r h)
5475	      left_inv := fun ψ => by
5476	        -- `grPointOfRankQuotient (ψ^* (U, u)) = ψ`: chart-locally, the pulled-back
5477	        -- matrix of sections is the `ψ`-image of the universal one, so the glued
5478	        -- morphism is `ψ` by uniqueness over the chart cover.
5479	        exact grPointOfRankQuotient_rqPullback_tautological d r ψ
5480	      right_inv := fun q => by
5481	        -- `(grPointOfRankQuotient x)^* (U, u) ~ x`: over each chart locus both
5482	        -- quotients are presented by the chart matrix, so the two `Abelian.epiDesc`
5483	        -- descents witness the equivalence (no descent gluing needed).
5484	        induction q using Quotient.ind with
5485	        | _ x => exact Quotient.sound (rqPullback_grPointOfRankQuotient_rel d r x) }
5486	  homEquiv_comp {T T'} f g := by
5487	    -- pseudofunctoriality of `rqPullback` at the tautological point — this is
5488	    -- `(functor d r).map_comp` evaluated at `⟦(U, u)⟧`
5489	    have h := congrArg (fun m => m (Quotient.mk _ (tautologicalRankQuotient d r)))
5490	      ((functor d r).map_comp g.op f.op)
5491	    exact h
5492	
5493	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

# Lean Audit Report

## Slug
iter080

## Iteration
080

## Scope
- files audited: 2
- files skipped (per directive): 0

---

## Per-file checklist

### AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GlueDescent.lean

- **outdated comments**: 2 flagged
- **suspect definitions**: none
- **dead-end proofs**: none
- **bad practices**: none
- **excuse-comments**: none
- **notes**:
  - Line 34: Comment `"axiom-clean since iter-056"` — stale iteration-number reference. The claim
    (no `axiom`) is correct, but the iteration pin is meaningless context. Minor.
  - Lines 3333–3346: Docstring for `isIso_glueRestrictionHom` (line 3347) retains
    `"PROOF ROUTE (scoped iter-066, partially built)"` and a trailing
    `"Remaining work: construct β_ij (via …) and verify the three conditions."` block.
    The theorem is fully proved (no `sorry`, no `axiom`; proof runs lines 3362–3381).
    The "partially built / Remaining work" language claims open obligations that no longer
    exist. Misleads future readers into thinking the proof is incomplete. Major.
  - No `sorry` or `axiom` anywhere in the 3409-line file.
  - No `set_option maxHeartbeats` anywhere in the file.
  - Private helpers `comp4_solve_last`, `comp4_solve_mid`, `comp4_solve_front`,
    `comp5_rearrange`, `side_collapse_left`, `side_collapse_right`, `final_cancel`
    (and many more abstract-category tactic helpers) — all used; none dead-end.
  - `glue`, `glueLift`, `glueRestrictionIso`, `isIso_glueRestrictionHom` — all proved,
    all connected in the dependency graph.

---

### AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean

- **outdated comments**: 3 flagged
- **suspect definitions**: none
- **dead-end proofs**: none (the one sorry is honestly open; see below)
- **bad practices**: 1 flagged (maxHeartbeats block at line 1059)
- **replicated private lemmas**: 2 groups (8 lemmas total)
- **excuse-comments**: none
- **notes**:
  - **Residual sorry (confirmed honest):** Line 2469,
    `theorem tautologicalQuotient_epi (d r : ℕ) : Epi (tautologicalQuotient d r) := by sorry`.
    Preceded by a detailed ROUTE comment (lines 2456–2468) explaining the proof strategy
    and naming the missing ingredient ("the joint-reflection lemma"). The sorry is
    intentional and correctly placed. `tautologicalRankQuotient` (line 2475) uses
    `tautologicalQuotient_epi d r` in its `epi` field — load-bearing and correctly so;
    the downstream `represents` theorem (line 5469) ultimately depends on it through
    `tautologicalRankQuotient`. No laundering of this sorry was found (no alias, no
    `noncomputable def`, no `Classical.choice` substitute).
  - **Stale "not yet formalized" on complete proof (line 2621):** Docstring for
    `chartLocus_isOpenCover` (line 2632) contains
    `"PROOF ROUTE (scoped iter-067, not yet formalized):"`.
    The proof is complete and spans lines 2632–2852 with 0 sorry. The "not yet formalized"
    note is a historical artefact. Major.
  - **Stale iter-ref (line 443):** Docstring for `bundleTransition_self` contains
    `"Resource note (iter-060)"`. The content is correct; the iteration pin is noise. Minor.
  - **Stale iter-ref (lines 2456–2468):** ROUTE comment for `tautologicalQuotient_epi`
    contains `"scoped iter-066"`. The sorry is honestly open; the iter-pin is stale. Minor.
  - **maxHeartbeats without explicit attribution (line 1059):**
    `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` for `pullback_map_freeMap_pullbackFreeIso`.
    The surrounding docstring describes the proof approach, but there is no inline `--`
    comment immediately before the `set_option` line explaining *why* the heartbeat budget
    needs raising (e.g. the cost driver). Every other `maxHeartbeats` block in the file
    (lines 1020, 1630, 1795, 1857, 3079, 3701, 3842, 3920, 3988, 4110, 4184, 4509, 4619,
    4937, 5110, 5148, 5195, 5236) carries an inline attribution comment. Minor.
  - **Private lemma replication — group 1 (lines 503–638):** 7 private lemmas with trailing
    `'`-names (`mul_submatrix_col'`, `map_nonsing_inv'`, `map_map_eq_of_comp'`,
    `isUnit_algebraMap_away_left'`, `inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel'`, `imageMatrix_map_eq'`,
    `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq'`) — verbatim or near-verbatim ports of private declarations
    from `GrassmannianCells.lean`. Comment at line 498 acknowledges this:
    `"Port of GrassmannianCells.X (private there)"`. The correct fix is to make these
    non-private in `GrassmannianCells.lean` or relocate them to a shared helper file. Major.
  - **Private lemma replication — group 2 (line 4026):** `matrixMap_nonsing_inv` is
    explicitly declared `"Project-local (copy of the private helper in GrassmannianCells.lean)"`.
    Same issue as group 1. Major.
  - **Documented non-private duplication (line 4038):** `imageMatrix_map_ringHom` is
    described as `"Non-private CommRing form of the helper inside GrassmannianCells.lean"` —
    a genuine CommRing generalisation rather than a verbatim copy. Should still be sourced in
    a shared location eventually. Minor.
  - **Documented verbatim proof copy (line 4070):** `chart_point_eq` notes `"proof copied
    verbatim"` from `existence_chart_kpoint_eq`. The `Field` hypothesis of the original is
    claimed to be unused. The generalisation is correct and the copy is documented. Minor.
  - Lines 1–2468 and 2470–5493: no additional `sorry` or `axiom`. All closed proofs are
    genuinely closed.
  - The `biproduct_matrix_comp` / `biproduct_matrix_comp_rect` / `biproduct_matrix_comp_rect₂`
    trio (lines 157, 254, 1150) each handles a different index shape; all are used. Not
    dead-end.

---

## Must-fix-this-iter

None.

---

## Major

- `GlueDescent.lean:3333` — Docstring for `isIso_glueRestrictionHom` retains `"PROOF ROUTE
  (scoped iter-066, partially built)"` and `"Remaining work: construct β_ij …"`. The proof
  is complete (0 sorry). Remove or replace with a "PROOF SUMMARY" note.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:2621` — Docstring for `chartLocus_isOpenCover` retains
  `"PROOF ROUTE (scoped iter-067, not yet formalized):"`. The proof is complete (0 sorry,
  lines 2632–2852). Remove the "not yet formalized" qualifier.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:503` — 7 private lemmas ported verbatim from `GrassmannianCells.lean`
  (group 1). Should be made non-private in GrassmannianCells.lean or moved to a shared file;
  until then the duplication is a maintenance hazard.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:4026` — `matrixMap_nonsing_inv` explicitly declared as a copy of a
  private helper in `GrassmannianCells.lean` (group 2). Same resolution as group 1.

---

## Minor

- `GlueDescent.lean:34` — Stale comment `"axiom-clean since iter-056"`. Remove the iter-pin.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:443` — Stale `"Resource note (iter-060)"` in `bundleTransition_self`
  docstring. Remove the iter-pin.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:2456` — ROUTE comment for `tautologicalQuotient_epi` includes
  `"scoped iter-066"`. The sorry is honestly open; the iter-pin is stale. Remove.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:1059` — `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` for
  `pullback_map_freeMap_pullbackFreeIso` lacks an inline attribution comment (all other
  `maxHeartbeats` blocks in the file have one). Add a short `-- <reason>` before the
  `set_option` line.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:4038` — `imageMatrix_map_ringHom` documented as a CommRing form
  of a private helper in GrassmannianCells.lean. Low risk; note for future factoring.
- `GrassmannianQuot.lean:4070` — `chart_point_eq` proof described as "copied verbatim".
  Low risk; consider upstreaming the CommRing generalisation to GrassmannianCells.lean.

---

## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)

None found. No comment in either file qualifies as an excuse-comment (no "TODO: replace with
real def", "placeholder", "temporary wrong", "will fix later" patterns).

---

## Severity summary

- **must-fix-this-iter**: 0
- **major**: 4
- **minor**: 6
- **excuse-comments**: 0

Overall verdict: Both files are sorry-clean except for the one honestly-open `tautologicalQuotient_epi` (declared as the residual sorry in the directive); no laundering was found; no axioms. The four major issues are stale "proof-in-progress" docstrings on two complete proofs and a code-duplication pattern (private lemma replication from `GrassmannianCells.lean`) that is acknowledged but unresolved across 8 instances.
